title: Eyelid bump
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: A stye is an inflamed oil gland at the edge of the eyelid and can appear as a red, swollen, tender bump.
Warm compresses are a common home-care measure for eyelid bumps.
Patients should seek clinician care for worsening swelling, vision problems, eye pain, fever, or failure to improve.
